_comment: Source: Michigan MDHHS Section 1-10 Foreign Body Airway Obstruction (Revised 05/08/2023, MDHHS Reviewed 2023). Base Michigan template — MCA-specific selections not configured.
protocol_reference: Michigan General Treatment Protocol Section 1-10: Foreign Body Airway Obstruction — MDHHS Approved 5/8/23, Reviewed 2023

sections.title: Foreign Body Airway Obstruction (FBAO)
sections.reference: Michigan Protocol 1-10: Foreign Body Airway Obstruction
points: IMPORTANT: Sudden cardiac arrest occurring while a person is eating is frequently dispatched as 'choking' — EMS personnel should consider these cases to be potential cardiac arrests
In conscious (responsive) adults and children > 1 year of age: deliver abdominal thrusts in rapid sequence until obstruction is relieved

Administer chest thrusts in conscious patients IN PLACE OF abdominal thrusts when: abdominal thrusts are ineffective (optional consideration); patient is obese and rescuer unable to encircle patient's abdomen; patient is in later stages of pregnancy (> 20 weeks); patient is under 1 year of age; wheelchair-bound patients

For conscious infants (under 1 year old) with evidence of severe FBAO: deliver repeated cycles of 5 back blows followed by 5 chest compressions until object is expelled or patient becomes unresponsive; NOTE: abdominal thrusts are NOT recommended for infants — may damage relatively large and unprotected liver
If any patient becomes unresponsive OR is found unresponsive and is unable to be ventilated using 2-person BVM technique with oropharyngeal airway: start CPR
For unconscious patients, while chest compressions are being provided: perform direct laryngoscopy; if foreign body is visible, remove using adult or pediatric Magill forceps (Specialist skill)
If unsuccessful in visualizing foreign body: continue chest compressions and repeat direct laryngoscopy while alternating with attempts to ventilate
Once FBAO is relieved, if spontaneous respiration does not return: refer to Airway Management-Procedure Protocol

deterioration_flags: Patient loses consciousness — start CPR immediately; proceed to direct laryngoscopy with Magill forceps (Specialist skill)
Unable to ventilate after patient is unresponsive — continue CPR; reassess airway with each cycle; attempt direct laryngoscopy
Obstruction not relieved with abdominal thrusts — alternate with chest thrusts; consider patient position and rescuer technique
scope_notes: Abdominal thrusts, back blows, chest thrusts: all levels
BVM ventilation with OPA: all levels
Direct laryngoscopy and Magill forceps: Specialist (AEMT/Paramedic) skill
Airway management procedures: refer to Airway Management-Procedure Protocol for scope by level
